id.instructions: Unique identifier for AI use case. Use cases should maintain the same ID if included in previous year's inventory. However, in their public inventories, agencies are permitted to remove the "Use Case ID" column. Required: Yes
id.description: Unique identifier for the use case

use_case_name.instructions: Title of the AI use case. Required: Yes

agency_bureau.instructions: Specific organization(s) within the agency responsible for the AI use case. Required: Yes

contact_email.instructions: Email address of the individual or team who works with or is responsible for the AI use case. However, in their public inventories, agencies may replace the "Email Address" column with an email address designated for public inquiries. Required: Yes
contact_email.description: Email Address for Point of Contact

is_withheld.instructions: Whether the AI use case should be withheld from public reporting and the reason for why. Required: Yes
valid_selections: b) Yes – agency has determined that there’s a risk to disclosure, such as a harm to an interest protected by a FOIA exemption
c) Yes – disclosure is prohibited by law

development_stage.instructions: The AI use case's current stage of development. Required: Yes
valid_selections: a) Pre-deployment – The use case is in a development or acquisition status.
b) Pilot – The use case has been deployed in a limited test or pilot capacity.
c) Deployed – The use case is being actively authorized or utilized to support the functions or mission of an agency.
d) Retired – The use case was reported in the agency’s prior year’s inventory, but its development and/or use has since been discontinued.

is_high_impact.excel_header: Is the AI use case high-impact?

b) Presumed high-impact, but determined: Presumed high-impact, but determined not high impact
b) Presumed high-impact but determined not high-impact

is_high_impact.instructions: Whether the AI use case is high-impact, as defined in Section 5 of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21. Required: Yes
valid_selections: b) Presumed high-impact, but determined not high impact

HI_justification.instructions: Provide a justification for why the use case is determined to be not high-impact. Required: Yes, but question should only appear if "Presumed high-impact, but determined not high-impact" is selected for the previous question.

Service Delivery: This potentially crosses all EPA business lines

topic_area.instructions: The topic area that most closely aligns with the AI use case. Required: Yes, for pre-deployment, pilot and deployed use cases.
topic_area.description: Topic area, required only if active

Computer Vision: AI that processes and i: Computer Vision - AI that processes and interprets visual data (e.g.; images and videos).

classification.instructions: The classification of AI technology that most closely aligns with the AI use case. Note that an AI use case may rely on multiple classifications; however, agencies should select the choice that most closely aligns. Required: Yes, for pre-deployment, pilot and deployed use cases.
valid_selections: Agentic AI: AI systems that perform tasks or make decisions autonomously with minimal human intervention.
Classical/Predictive Machine Learning: Models trained on data to make predictions or classifications based on identified patterns or relationships.
Computer Vision: AI that processes and interprets visual data (e.g., images and videos).
Generative AI: AI that generates new or synthetic content (e.g., images, videos, audio, text, code).
Natural Language Processing: AI that processes, interprets, and shares information in human language.
Reinforcement Learning: AI trained through trial and error using rewards and penalties to optimize decision-making policies.

problem_solved.excel_header: What problem is the AI intended to solve?
problem_solved.instructions: What problem is the AI intended to solve? Required: Yes, for pre-deployment, pilot and deployed use cases.

benefits.excel_header: What are the expected benefits and positive outcomes from the AI for an agency’s mission and/or the general public?
benefits.instructions: Description of the expected benefits and positive outcomes from the AI for an agency's mission and/or the general public. Expected benefits may include, for example: cost savings, reducing customer wait times, or improving access to government benefits. Required: Yes, for pre-deployment, pilot and deployed use cases.

system_outputs.instructions: Description of what the AI system outputs, whether it's a prediction, recommendation, decision, etc. Required: Yes, for pre-deployment, pilot and deployed use cases.
system_outputs.description: Technical description of what the AI produces

operational_date.excel_header: Date when AI use case became operational or the pilot’s start date
operational_date.instructions: Date when the AI use case was fully operational or the pilot's start date. Required: Yes, for pilot and deployed use cases.

contracting_usage.excel_header: Was the system involved in this use case purchased from a vendor or developed under contract(s) or in-house?

b) Developed in-house: No contract/external resources used in development.
c) Developed with both contracting and i: Developed with both contracting and in-house resources
Developed with both contracting and in-house resources
Developed with a combination of in-house and contract/external resources.
Both, the AI was developed by a vendor but it was substantially customized in-house or by a contractor

contracting_usage.instructions: Whether the system involved in the use case was developed, or is expected to be developed, exclusively with contracting resources, in-house, or a combination of both. Required: Yes, for pilot and deployed use cases.
valid_selections: c) Developed with both contracting and in-house resources
contracting_usage.description: How much contracting was used to build system

vendor_name.instructions: Identification of the vendor(s) involved in the use case's development and/or maintenance. Required: Yes, but question should only appear if "Purchased from a vendor" or "Developed with both contracting and in-house resources" is selected for the previous question and if development stage is pilot or deployed.

have_ato.excel_header: Does this AI use case have an associated Authorization to Operate (ATO)?

Yes: Use vendor's ATO or FedRAMP authorization

have_ato.instructions: Whether the AI use case itself has an associated Authorization to Operate (ATO), or is part of another system that has an ATO. Required: Yes, for pilot and deployed use cases.

system_name_ato.instructions: Name of the system(s) associated with this AI use case, according to the ATO. Required: Yes, but question should only appear if "Yes" is selected for the previous question and development stage is pilot or deployed.
system_name_ato.description: Name associated with system if system has an ATO

data_description.excel_header: Describe any data used to  train, fine-tune, and/or  evaluate performance of the  model(s) used in this use  case.
data_description.instructions: General description of the data used in the AI's design, development, training, testing, and operation. This should include a description of any data provided by the agency, whether the datasets are research datasets, publicly available, external, etc. and to the extent possible, sufficient descriptive information from the vendor. Required: Yes, for pilot and deployed use cases.
data_description.description: Details on training and evaluation data

link_to_data.excel_header: If the data is required to be  publicly disclosed as an  open government data asset,  provide a link to the entry on the Federal Data Catalog.
link_to_data.instructions: If the data is required to be publicly disclosed as an open government data asset, provide a link to the entry on the Federal Data Catalog. Required: No
link_to_data.description: Link to data on Federal Data Catalog

has_pii.excel_header: Does this AI use case  involve personally  identifiable information (PII)  that is maintained by the  agency?

Yes: Yes (incidental PII may be involved through authenticated platform users)
Yes - System logs may contain user identifiers
Yes - Customer contact and account information
Yes - Loan applicant and business owner PII
Yes - Business and individual information in audit records
No: No - General content generation and research
No - Code and technical documentation only

has_pii.instructions: Whether the AI use case involves personally identifiable information (PII), as defined in OMB Circular A-130. Required: Yes, for pilot and deployed use cases.

pia_url.excel_header: If publicly available, provide  the link to the AI use case’s associated Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA).
pia_url.instructions: Whether the AI use case itself has an associated PIA, or is part of another system that has a PIA. Required: No

demographic_features.excel_header: Which, if any, demographic  variables does the AI use  case explicitly use as model  features?
demographic_features.instructions: Identification of the demographic features explicitly utilized in the AI use case's data/model(s). Required: Yes, for pilot and deployed use cases.
demographic_features.description: Demographic variables used in the model

has_custom_code.excel_header: Does this project include  custom-developed code?
has_custom_code.instructions: Whether this AI project includes "custom developed code," as defined in OMB Memorandum M-25-21. Required: Yes, for pilot and deployed use cases.

code_url.excel_header: If the code is open source, provide the link for the  publicly available source  code.
code_url.instructions: Link to publicly available source code for the AI use case, if available. Required: No

hi_testing_conducted.excel_header:
Has pre-deployment testing been conducted for this AI  use case? 




Practice: Complete AI Impact Assessment
hi_testing_conducted.instructions: Whether pre-deployment testing has been conducted for the AI use case, consistent with Section 4(b)(i) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21. Required: Yes, only for high-impact deployed use cases
valid_selections: c) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

hi_assessment_completed.excel_header:
Has an AI impact assessment been completed for this AI use case? 



Practice: Complete AI Impact Assessment

c) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum p: Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

hi_assessment_completed.instructions: Whether an AI impact assessment for the AI use case has been completed, consistent with Section 4(b)(ii) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25- 21. Required: Yes, only for high-impact deployed use cases
valid_selections: c) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB
hi_assessment_completed.description: High Impact: Impact assessment completion flag

hi_potential_impacts.excel_header:
What are the potential  impacts of using the AI for  this particular use case and  how were they identified?    



Subpractice: Complete AI  Impact Assessment
hi_potential_impacts.instructions: Identification of the reasonably foreseeable impacts from using the AI use case, to include impacts on the privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties of the public. Required: Yes, only for high-impact deployed use cases
hi_potential_impacts.description: High Impact: Description of potential impacts

hi_independent_review.excel_header:
Has as independent review  of the AI use case been  conducted?    




Sub practice Complete AI  Impact Assessment

a) Yes – by another appropriate agency o: yes – by another appropriate agency office or reviewer not directly involved in the ai's development
b) Yes – by an agency AI oversight board: Yes – by an agency AI oversight board not directly involved in the AI’s development
e) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum p: Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

hi_independent_review.instructions: Whether an independent review of the AI use case has been conducted by an office, oversight board, or individual within the agency who has not been involved in the AI's development.Required: Yes, only for high-impact deployed use cases
valid_selections: a) Yes – by another appropriate agency office or reviewer not directly involved in the AI’s development
b) Yes – by an agency AI oversight board not directly involved in the AI’s development
e) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

Is there a process to conduct
ongoing monitoring to
identify any adverse impacts
to the performance and
security of the AI functionality, as well as to privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties?

Practice: Conduct Ongoing Monitoring for Performance and Potential Adverse Impacts

a) Yes, sufficient monitoring protocols: Yes, sufficient monitoring protocols have been established
b) Development of monitoring protocols i: Development of monitoring protocols is in-progess

valid_selections: a) Yes, sufficient monitoring protocols have been established
b) Development of monitoring protocols is in-progess

Has the agency established sufficient and periodic  training for operators of the  AI to interpret and act on the  its output and managed associated risks?

a) Yes, sufficient and periodic training: Yes, sufficient and periodic training has been established

b) Establishment of sufficient and periodic training is in-progress

hi_failsafe_presence.instructions: Whether the AI use case has an appropriate fail-safe that minimizes the risk of significant harm, consistent with Section 4(b)(v) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21. Required: Yes, only for high-impact deployed use cases
valid_selections: d) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

hi_appeal_process.excel_header:
Is there an established appeal process in the event that an impacted individual would like to appeal or  contest the AI system’s  outcome?

a) Yes, an appropriate appeal process ha: Yes, an appropriate appeal process has been established
c) Establishment of an appropriate appea: Establishment of an appropriate appeal process is in-progress

hi_appeal_process.instructions: Whether there is an established appeal process for this AI use case in the event that an impacted individual would like to appeal or contest the AI system's outcome, consistent with Section 4(b)(vi) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21. Required: Yes, only for high-impact deployed use cases
